title_en: A very silly adaptation of “The Odyssey”
title_zh: 《奥德赛》一部颇为荒诞的改编
rubric_en: Sir Christopher Nolan’s film is emblematic of its time
rubric_zh: 克里斯托弗·诺兰的这部电影代表了它那个时代

paragraphs.en: The squabbles have been epic. Sir Christopher Nolan’s adaptation of “The Odyssey” was causing conniptions long before anyone had even seen the thing. Ahead of its release on July 17th, there was harrumphing over everything from the actress playing Helen (too black); the colour of the plume on Odysseus’s helmet (too red); the soldiers’ armour (also too black, and too Batman-like) and the vocabulary of Odysseus’s son (too American: at one point he says “Dad”). On YouTube the film’s trailer was met with criticism. “If I saw this on a plane, I’d still walk out,” reads one disgruntled comment.
paragraphs.zh: 争议大得惊人。克里斯托弗·诺兰改编的《奥德赛》在正式上映之前就已经引发了轰动。影片定于7月17日上映前，争议便从女主海伦的选角（太黑了）、奥德修斯头盔上的羽饰颜色（太红了）、士兵的铠甲（也太黑、太像蝙蝠侠）到奥德修斯儿子的用词（太美国化：有一处他说“Dad”）无所不包。影片预告片在YouTube上也遭到批评。一条不满评论写道：“如果我在飞机上看这部片，我仍然会下飞机。”

paragraphs.en: The film itself is unlikely to allay any anxieties about anachronisms. In everything from its scenery to its psychology it says far more about the modern West than about ancient Greece. Startling liberties are taken with the plot to turn Odysseus, the archetypally flawed ancient hero, into a simple modern goody by flattening his flaws. Any bad behaviour is explained away either by drug use or hinted to be due to proto-PTSD (the overdiagnosis epidemic having apparently reached the ancient shores of Ithaca). The ending, which has him pierced by arrows and turned into a St Sebastian-style pincushion, is sillier still.
paragraphs.zh: 影片本身也不太可能平息人们对时代错置的担忧。它从布景到人物心理几乎都更像在诉说当代西方，而非古希腊。为了把奥德修斯这个典型的“有缺陷”古典英雄改造成一个单纯的现代正面形象，影片在情节上做出了令人震惊的取舍，压平了他的缺点。影片把任何不当行为都归咎于吸毒，或暗示源于一种“早期PTSD”（似乎“过度诊断”已一并漂到了伊萨卡的古老海岸）。结局里他被箭射中后化作“圣塞巴斯蒂安式”穿针刺绣的靶盘般的样子，更是荒诞离谱。

paragraphs.en: The scenery is similarly unsuitable, and often far too pretty. Soldiers in elegant gunmetal grey look less like ancient warriors than Warhammer toys. The ochre, candlelit palaces do not resemble warlords’ lairs but boutique hotels. During the beach scenes with the nymph Calypso (Charlize Theron), Odysseus (Matt Damon) seems less to be in agonising exile than on a Caribbean mini-break (they are wearing lots of linen). And the less said about the Cyclops, the better.
paragraphs.zh: 布景同样不合适，而且常常太过好看。身披雅致枪灰色铠甲的士兵，看上去不像古代勇士，更像是战锤（Warhammer）模型玩具。黄褐色、烛光照亮的宫殿，也不像霸主的隐居营寨，更像精品酒店。奥德修斯和海神女（卡吕普索）在海滩上的片段（女主由查莉泽·塞隆饰演，奥德修斯由马特·达蒙饰演）更像是去加勒比海度了个短途度假（两人都穿着大量亚麻布），而不是在承受痛苦的放逐。对独眼巨人的描写越少提及越好。

paragraphs.en: This is irksome but typical. Every adaptation of an ancient text is as much about its own era as the original work; less a lens than a mirror. The ancient Athenians transformed Homeric epic into psychologically acute tragedy. In the 18th century Alexander Pope’s translation managed to make Odysseus sound like an effete Enlightenment poet rather than an ancient warrior-king. In 1922 James Joyce’s version offered sex, stream of consciousness and that Modernist literary staple: boredom.
paragraphs.zh: 这种情况令人恼火，却并不罕见。每一次古典文本的改编，本质上都和原作一样，反映的是自身时代；它更像一面镜子，而非一只镜片。古代雅典人把荷马史诗改写成了心理深刻的悲剧。18世纪，亚历山大·蒲柏的译本让奥德修斯听起来像一位文雅的启蒙时代诗人，而不是一位古代的武士国王。1922年，詹姆斯·乔伊斯的版本则加入了性、意识流，以及现代主义文学的经典元素：无聊。

paragraphs.en: In this way, Sir Christopher’s retelling is part of a time-honoured tradition. It is manifestly a product of the present day. Female characters offer frequent apologias for feminism (not a noted strong suit of the ancients). The casus belli of the Trojan war is given as a desire to improve trade routes, as if Agamemnon were merely a blameless advocate for free trade rather than one of antiquity’s greatest thugs.
paragraphs.zh: 在这个意义上，克里斯托弗·诺兰的重述属于一项历史悠久的传统。它显然是当下时代的产物。女角色频繁为女权主义发声（这并非古人所擅长的强项）。特洛伊战争的开战理由被说成是为了改善贸易路线，仿佛阿伽门农只是一个无可指摘的自由贸易倡导者，而不是古代最凶狠的恶霸之一。

paragraphs.en: Adaptations have some latitude, for “The Odyssey” contains multitudes. In one of the most famous scenes, the god Proteus transforms into a lion, then a snake, then rushing water. “The Odyssey” is more protean still. In the three millennia since it was composed, it has been on an odyssey all its own.
paragraphs.zh: 改编自《奥德赛》有一定的解释空间，因为《奥德赛》本身内涵丰富。 在最著名的一个场景中，海神波塞冬化作一头狮子，又化作一条蛇，接着变成奔腾的水流。《奥德赛》本身更具多变性。在它成书后的三千年里，它一直经历着属于自己的一次“奥德赛式”漂流与变形。

paragraphs.en: The poem has become a noun (an odyssey) and an adjective (Odyssean). It has become a play, a TV series, a novel (“Ulysses”) and a porn film (“Odyssey: The Ultimate Trip”). It has become a lunar lander (IM-1 Odysseus), a large asteroid (1143 Odysseus) and a body lotion (which, with notes of black pepper and sandalwood, no doubt smells better than its namesake would have).
paragraphs.zh: 这部史诗已经成为一个名词（odyssey），也成为一个形容词（Odyssean）。它又变成一部戏剧、一部电视剧、一部小说（《尤利西斯》）和一部色情电影（《Odyssey: The Ultimate Trip》）。它还成为一艘登月器（IM-1 Odysseus）、一颗大行星（1143 Odysseus）以及一款润肤乳（带有黑胡椒和檀香气味，毫无疑问闻起来会比它的名字来源更好闻）。

paragraphs.en: It is therefore not surprising that Odysseus, the sacker of cities, has been transformed once again by Sir Christopher. In other ways it is surprising, as “The Odyssey” has been a wrecker of reputations. In 2024 a film starring Ralph Fiennes was dismissed as “dull” and a “slog”. A movie of 1954, starring Kirk Douglas, was described as “weird” and “vapid”.
paragraphs.zh: 所以，战神奥德修斯（Odysseus）再次被克里斯托弗改造成别样形态并不令人意外。另一些方面则更令人意外，因为《奥德赛》历来是“毁名作”——它毁掉过不少作品的评价。在2024年，一部由拉尔夫·费因斯主演的电影被批评为“枯燥”与“漫长乏味”。一部1954年的电影，主角是柯克·道格拉斯，也被描述为“怪异”和“空洞”。

paragraphs.en: Sir Christopher has form on tricky topics: his previous film, “Oppenheimer”, about particle physics and nuclear weapons, grossed almost $1bn at the box office. Still, he has learned that “The Odyssey” is not an easy book to film.
paragraphs.zh: 克里斯托弗·诺兰在处理棘手题材方面一向是得心应手：他上一部关于粒子物理和核武器的电影《奥本海默》在票房上几乎赚到10亿美元。不过，他也已经明白《奥德赛》并不是一部容易拍成电影的书。

paragraphs.en: That porn flick hints at why: it describes itself, with admirable artistic honesty, as “Three unrelated stories”. The original “Odyssey” is not much better. It tells the tale of Odysseus’s ten-year journey home from the Trojan war and thus is commonly thought of as a kind of watery Greek road trip. But it is also a sex-and-sea-monsters film, a father-and-son film and a husband-and-wife film.
paragraphs.zh: 那部电影也从侧面提示了原因：它用可敬的艺术诚实自称是“三个不相关的故事”。原著《奥德赛》也不算容易处理。它讲述了奥德修斯从特洛伊战争归乡、历时十年的旅程，因此常被当作一种“泼天”希腊公路片来看待；但它同时也是一部性与海怪题材的片子，一部父子关系电影，也是一部夫妻关系电影。

paragraphs.en: Its structure is fiendishly twisty. It is about Odysseus—but readers do not meet him for four books. Moreover, many of the stories that you think of as archetypally Odyssean are either not told at all or told in brisk narrative flashback: the Sirens slip by in some 60 lines and the Lotus-Eaters in about 20. It is, as Sir Christopher said, “the original non-linear narrative”. It is, says Emily Wilson, a classicist who published a dazzling translation in 2017, “complicated”.
paragraphs.zh: 它的结构狡猾得令人头疼。它写的是奥德修斯，但读者前四卷都见不到他。另外，许多我们认为是典型“奥德赛式”的故事，要么根本没被讲述，要么只在叙事回闪中匆匆一带而过：海妖情节大约只用60行，罗托斯食人花（神话中的“食莲花者”）也只占约20行。诺兰本人曾说，这是一种“最早的非线性叙事”；而2017年出版过一部极具亮点译本的古典文学学者艾米莉·威尔逊（Emily Wilson）则称它“复杂”。

paragraphs.en: It is also very famous. The second work of Western literature, it was once assumed to be secondary in seriousness to its more manly prequel, “The Iliad”. Yet in recent years its fame has far eclipsed it. The first Penguin Classic ever printed was “The Odyssey”. The pyramid of intellectual biomass resting upon it is vast. It is only 12,000 lines long, but look up “Odyssey” in the British Library and you are offered 34,000 titles: almost three for every line. “Iliad” returns a mere 9,000.
paragraphs.zh: 它也非常出名。作为西方文学中的第二部作品，它曾被认为严肃性上不及更“阳刚”的前作《伊利亚特》。但近几年，它的名声已经远远盖过了后者。企鹅经典（Penguin Classic）第一本付印的书就是《奥德赛》。围绕它建立起来的思想金字塔非常庞大。它只有12,000行，但在英国图书馆里检索“Odyssey”，会出现34,000个书目：几乎每一行都对应三条记录；而“Iliad”只有9,000条。

paragraphs.en: That it has become yet another film—and created yet more debates—is further proof of its permanence. There are two ways to see such squabbles. One is to engage with them on their own terms. These gripes have some validity: in Homer, Helen is clearly described as “white-armed”; Odysseus probably didn’t wear a red-plumed helmet (red was a Spartan habit); ancient Greek armour was bronze, not Batman-ish; and Odysseus’s son definitely didn’t say “Dad”. As Tim Whitmarsh, Regius professor of Greek at Cambridge University, points out: “The ancient Greeks spoke Greek.”
paragraphs.zh: 它又成为另一部电影，并引发了更多争论，这反而进一步证明了它的持久性。对这些争执可以有两种解读。第一种是按争执本身来回应：这些抱怨确有一定道理。荷马的文本中，海伦确实被明确写作“白臂（白纤手）”；奥德修斯大概没有戴着红羽毛头盔（红色是斯巴达人的习俗）；古希腊的盔甲是青铜的，而不是“蝙蝠侠式”的；而且奥德修斯的儿子绝对没有说“Dad”。正如剑桥大学拉吉厄斯希腊语教授蒂姆·惠特马什（Tim Whitmarsh）指出的：“古希腊人说的是希腊语。”

paragraphs.en: The other way to see these arguments is as proof of the malleability of “The Odyssey”. In an era in which people bristle at American influence in Europe, it lets people bristle at Americanisms (“Dad!”) in ancient Greece. In an era anxious about the excessively manly manosphere, people can fret over excessively manly armour. In an era riven by debates about race, people have engaged in bad-tempered rows over casting. We are not arguing about “The Odyssey” when we argue about “The Odyssey”. We are arguing about ourselves.
paragraphs.zh: 另一种看法是，把这些争论视作《奥德赛》可塑性的证明。在一个对欧洲受美国影响神经紧绷的时代，它允许人们对古希腊中的“美国式”说法（“Dad!”）生气；在一个对“男子气概”过盛的男性话语圈（manosphere）焦虑的时代，人们会对过于“男性化”的盔甲斤斤计较；在一个被种族争论撕扯的时代，人们则围绕选角爆发激烈争吵。我们在讨论《奥德赛》时并不是在辩论《奥德赛》本身。我们其实在辩论的是我们自己。

paragraphs.en: Each generation remakes “The Odyssey” in its own image: you get the version you deserve. The clever, quarrelsome ancient Athenians took Homer and transformed him into clever, quarrelsome tragedy. The Modernists turned him into Modernist prose and poetry. The simplistic, visual modern world has taken Homer and turned him into social-media spats and an absurd Cyclops—and thus into a tragedy that is all its own. ■
paragraphs.zh: 每一代人都在按自己的样子重塑《奥德赛》：你看到的是你所应得的版本。聪明而爱争辩的古雅典人把荷马改造成了聪明而爱争辩的悲剧。现代主义者把他改写成了现代主义散文和诗歌。而这个简化、视觉化的现代世界，将荷马变成了社交媒体上的口角和一场荒诞的独眼巨人故事——于是《奥德赛》也就变成了一部属于这个时代自己的悲剧。■

paragraphs.en: For more on the latest books, films, TV shows, albums and controversies, sign up to Plot Twist, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter
paragraphs.zh: 如需了解更多最新图书、电影、电视剧、专辑和争议，欢迎订阅 Plot Twist，每周一期、仅向订阅者开放。
